attractions.name: Agios Andreas Beach Katakolon
attractions.description: A simple, peaceful beach where you can dip your feet in the water or just relax by the shore

attractions.name: Katakolon Main Street
attractions.description: A quaint street lined with souvenir shops, local boutiques, and small cafés- perfect for a gentle start to your stop

phrases: Πόσο κοστίζει; (POH-so ko-STEE-zee) — How much does it cost?
Dress modestly when visiting nearby ancient Olympia or any churches - cover shoulders and knees
Greeks typically eat lunch late (2-4 PM) and dinner very late (9-11 PM), so plan accordingly
Tipping 10-15% at restaurants is appreciated, and rounding up taxi fares is customary
Don't wave with an open palm facing outward - it's considered offensive in Greek culture
safety_tips: INDIAN EMBASSY DETAILS: Indian Embassy in Athens - Address: 4 Kleanthous Street, 10674 Athens, Greece. Phone: +30-210-7216227/7257138. Emergency Contact: +30-6944-295-295. Email: cons.athens@mea.gov.in. Consular hours: Monday-Friday 9:00-13:00
EMERGENCY NUMBERS: Police - 100, Ambulance - 166, Fire Department - 199, Tourist Police - 171, European Emergency Number - 112. Port Authority Katakolon: +30-26210-41052
LOCAL POLICE: Katakolon Port Police: +30-26210-41052. Pyrgos Police Station (nearest main station): +30-26210-22100. Always carry identification documents
MEDICAL EMERGENCY: Nearest hospital is Pyrgos General Hospital (+30-26210-81100), 15km from Katakolon. Carry travel insurance documents and emergency medical information in English and Greek

COMMON SCAMS: Overcharging at restaurants near port - always check prices before ordering. Fake taxi meters - agree on fare beforehand. Overpriced souvenirs - compare prices at multiple shops. Unofficial tour guides - use licensed operators only

TRANSPORTATION SAFETY: Use only licensed taxis with official markings. Agree on fare before starting journey. For Olympia tours, book through cruise ship or verified tour operators. Train to Olympia is safe and affordable option

MONEY SAFETY: Use ATMs inside banks when possible. Avoid carrying large amounts of cash. Credit cards widely accepted. Be cautious of card skimming devices. Keep emergency cash separate from main wallet
FOOD AND WATER: Tap water is generally safe but bottled water recommended. Eat at busy restaurants with high turnover. Be cautious of seafood in extreme heat. Check restaurant hygiene standards
PERSONAL SAFETY: Stay in well-lit tourist areas. Don't leave belongings unattended on beaches. Be aware of pickpockets in crowded areas. Inform someone of your itinerary when exploring independently
COMMUNICATION: Download offline maps before exploring. Learn basic Greek phrases: 'Voithia' (Help), 'Astynomia' (Police), 'Nosokomeio' (Hospital). Keep embassy contact details saved in phone
WEATHER PRECAUTIONS: Summer temperatures can exceed 35°C - carry water and wear sun protection. Check weather conditions before beach activities. Strong winds can affect ferry schedules

CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Dress modestly when visiting religious sites. Remove hats and cover shoulders in churches. Photography may be restricted in some archaeological sites. Respect local customs and traditions
CRUISE SHIP COORDINATION: Always check ship departure times. Keep ship contact details handy. Inform ship's guest services of your plans. Return to ship at least 30 minutes before departure
DOCUMENT SECURITY: Keep passport copies separate from originals. Store digital copies in cloud storage. Carry ship ID card at all times. Report lost documents to embassy immediately

INTERNET SAFETY: Use secure WiFi networks only. Avoid accessing sensitive information on public networks. Keep phone charged for emergency communications. Consider purchasing local SIM card for better connectivity
connectivity_tips: Purchase a Cosmote or Vodafone Greece SIM card at Athens airport before heading to Katakolon - costs around €15-25 for tourist packages with 5-10GB data and works well in coastal areas
Buy local Greek SIM cards from OTE shops in nearby Pyrgos town (15km from Katakolon port) - offers better rates than airport purchases at €10-20 for prepaid plans
Consider eSIM options like Airalo or Holafly before traveling - Greece eSIM plans cost $8-15 for 1-3GB and can be activated instantly upon arrival
Wind Hellas (Tre) offers competitive tourist SIM packages at €12-18 with good coverage in Katakolon and surrounding archaeological sites
Download offline maps and translation apps before arrival as mobile coverage can be patchy in remote areas around Ancient Olympia (30km inland)
European roaming plans from Indian carriers like Airtel or Jio cost ₹500-1500 per day - more expensive but convenient for short cruise stops
Purchase SIM cards from kiosks near Katakolon port during cruise season - limited options but available for €20-30 with basic data packages
Cosmote generally offers the best network coverage in rural Peloponnese region including Katakolon - expect 4G speeds in town, 3G in countryside
Free WiFi is available at most cafes and restaurants in Katakolon port area - useful for messaging apps and basic internet needs
For day trips to Ancient Olympia, inform family about potential connectivity gaps as mobile signals weaken significantly in the archaeological site area
health_tips: No specific vaccinations are required for Indian travelers visiting Katakolon, Greece, as there are no mandatory vaccine requirements for entry from India.
Ensure routine vaccinations are up-to-date: MMR (measles, mumps, rubella), DPT (diphtheria, pertussis, tetanus), chickenpox, polio, and yearly flu shot.
Recommended vaccines include Hepatitis A and Hepatitis B for protection against foodborne and bloodborne infections.
Consider Typhoid vaccination if you plan to eat at local tavernas or street food, though risk is generally low in Greece.
Tetanus-diphtheria booster is recommended if your last shot was more than 10 years ago.
Tap water in Katakolon is generally safe to drink, but bottled water is widely available if you prefer or have a sensitive stomach.
Greek cuisine is generally safe - enjoy fresh seafood, but ensure it's properly cooked and served hot.
Be cautious with dairy products and salads at smaller establishments; stick to reputable restaurants.
Pack basic medications including anti-diarrheal medicine, pain relievers, and any prescription medications you regularly take.
Sun protection is essential - bring high SPF sunscreen, hat, and sunglasses as Mediterranean sun can be intense.
Greece has excellent healthcare facilities; EU citizens should carry EHIC card, others should ensure travel insurance covers medical expenses.
No malaria risk in Greece, so antimalarial medication is not needed.
Swimming in the Ionian Sea is generally safe, but be aware of occasional jellyfish and strong currents.
Insect repellent may be useful during summer months to prevent mosquito bites, though disease risk is minimal.
Emergency number in Greece is 112; most tourist areas have English-speaking medical staff.
